programmes.show_description: Love and anticipation are in the air at the Solana as Joyce and Monty's wedding day arrives - when things go awry at the wedding on Peacock Island, Kenneth volunteers to save the day. S10 E1
Terry has to mind Danny Varrow, a footballer whose gambling, womanising and boozing leaves his club in the lurch. S3 E11: Poetic Justice, Innit?
Alf has a fight at a tea dance and arranges to meet up again with his adversary outside his house. S3 E5

When Mildred enters a flower arranging competition, Chrissy offers her a plant from her garden; when Larry sees one in Chrissy's flat, he thinks it's a cannabis plant. S5 E5: How Does Your Garden Grow
David goes to a casino where he meets the Cochrans and hopes that Linda will bring him luck as he puts his winning system into play. S2 E2: Casino
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S6, E5: The Right Spirit
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S6, E6: A Question Of Taste

The Settle to Carlisle Railway, completed in 1876, is one of the most scenic and impressive railways in the UK with viaducts, tunnels and views of the North Pennines, Eden Valley, and Yorkshire Dales.

Mattresses and toppers from Dormeo - the home of a great night's sleep. Every body. Every bedroom. Every budget. Teleshopping.
Jet Hawk is cordless, rechargeable and turns any water source into a powerful pressure washer. Call or visit highstreettv.com for more details. Teleshopping.
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Classic comedy sketch show starring Dick Emery and featuring June Whitfield and Pat Coombs. S10 E1: Matrimonial Stakes
Kenneth Williams and Barbara Windsor introduce a compilation of the best scenes from 20 years' worth of Carry On films. Comedy 1977 (PG)
There's trouble on the buses as a strike hits the depot, new rotas mean Stan's on an earlier shift, and misses the canteen's lunch opening entirely. S1 E1: The Early Shift
Stan is always nervous when he has a new conductor but this time he is in for a pleasant surprise. S1 E2: The New Conductor
Stan's job is made more difficult when Olive becomes a clippie aboard his bus. S1 E3: Olive Takes a Trip
Stan's stomach pains are 'an occupational hazard' from sitting over a vibrating engine all day according to him, but Arthur has other ideas. S1 E4: Bus Drivers' Stomach
Stan applies for a promotion in hopes that he can buy his mum the new coat she needs, but he gets far more than just a raise when he's promoted to assistant inspector. S1 E5: The New Inspector
Stan is put in charge of organising a new cook when the management hand over running the depot canteen to the staff - Blakey is keen on his first appointment, but no-one else is. S1 E6: The Canteen
Stan and Jack have their masculinity threatened when they are challenged to a darts match by Iris and Jenny. S1 E7: The Darts Match
Ethel has fallen in love with an American pilot called Joe, but Wilfred is not at all pleased. S2 E6: Love is a Very Splendid Thing
The annual bowls club outing seems to provide an ideal opportunity for Cecil to be alone with May. S2 E7: A Bowl In The Hand
The station is prepared for a visit by the chairman of British Railways, but it turns out to be a different Doctor Beeching altogether. S2 E8: Action Stations
It's Jack and May's wedding anniversary, but May is worried that she might be pregnant - meanwhile, Arnold fears that he is about to get the sack. S2 E9: A Pregnant Pause
The staff discover that the station is a hundred years old and decide to have a street party to celebrate. S2 E10: Ton Up
It's 1962 and Gary Sparrow has been trapped in the past for 17 years, but his decision to witness his own birth sees him catapulted into the 21st century: Many Happy Returns (2016 Special)
Harold wants to go to Switzerland for Christmas whilst Albert - as usual - prefers Bognor; he is persuaded to go provided they sail rather than fly. S8 E7: A Perfect Christmas
Terry passes his driving test and gets a job as a minicab driver; on Christmas Eve, he drives Thelma and Bob to a party which leads to more trouble. S2 E14: A Special Christmas Edition
When Arthur, Dave and Ray take a trip across the English Channel to take advantage of duty-free prices, it's Arthur who ultimately gets taken advantage of. S10 E2: Another Case of Van Blank
The wedding party gets back after a messy night out with no sign of Kenneth, only for Jacqueline's concern to turn to joy when he turns up after 'the night of his life.' S10 E2
Desperate to make a good impression at her parents' party, Maxine cajoles Ian into pretending to be her boyfriend. When he agrees, deep feelings are uncovered, taking everyone by surprise. S7 E5
The youngsters want a party, but George - recalling the last one - is against the idea. Mildred, however, is all for it and gives it her blessing... S6 E1: The Party's Over
Health and fitness are the new buzzwords in the house when Rigsby's keenness to portray himself in prime condition leads him to challenge Philip to a boxing match. S2 E3: A Body Like Mine
The wedding party gets back after a messy night out with no sign of Kenneth, only for Jacqueline's concern to turn to joy when he turns up after 'the night of his life.' S10 E2
Terry's pleasure at seeing 'Oily' Wragg again is cut short by the appearance of some less friendly faces from the past. S3 E12: Back in Good Old England
Alf complains to the DHSS that he should have been paid more winter's heating allowance. He has a loud revealing dream in front of his friends. S3 E6
Chrissy receives two tickets for a Frank Sinatra concert but does not want them; she instead enjoys being pampered by Jo and Robin as they compete for the tickets. S5 E6: Come Fly With Me
When a young English couple arrive at the hotel, Amy and Linda assume that they are newlyweds because they are so evidently in love. S2 E3: Couples
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S6, E7: A Pair Of Bloomers
Cindernellie: Hit '70s sitcom with Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker. Nellie and Eli are left performing a pantomime after the producer does a runner with their money. Christmas 1972 (S7, E1)
We make the spectacular journey from Lancaster to Barrow-in-Furness on the historic and beautiful Furness Railway exploring its history, the area's mining heritage, and the sights along the way.
Make perfect toasties right in your microwave - that's right! Clever Micro Toastie is microwave cooking with a crunch! Order now at www.highstreettv.com. Teleshopping.

Arthur sells some goods to a Russian sailor when a ship arrives in port, but he swiftly finds himself involved in a diplomatic situation - and much worse off. S6 E5: From Fulham With Love
Richard is about to meet a new partner - is she all that she seems, is there a dark side to this women's brother, and are they both out to deceive Richard? S3 E6: Desirous of Change
When Joey has to tell his mother that he is in debt to the tune of £20,000, the whole family are required to help him out. S6 E3
When the Steptoes find out that the lease is running out on their house and need a bank loan to buy it, the bank manager ends up making them an offer they can't refuse. S5 E7: Men of Property
Jack is convinced that something is going on between May and Cecil - and with good reason as they knew each other 18 years earlier and May happens to have an 18-year-old daughter. S2 E2: Father's Day
Health and fitness are the new buzzwords in the house when Rigsby's keenness to portray himself in prime condition leads him to challenge Philip to a boxing match. S2 E3: A Body Like Mine
When George has a nasty cold and gives it to Robin, Chrissy and Jo try to make him better using a range of home-made remedies. S1 E6: Match of the Day
Gary's world is changing fast. Yvonne is changing careers as Phoebe plans the wedding. And Reg's domestic problems threaten his job. S4 E7: Easy Living
Tom is released from prison; however, there is going to be trouble when a Scottish stranger comes looking for him and Jack. S2 E2: Tram Ride to the Bluebell
Arthur sells some goods to a Russian sailor when a ship arrives in port, but he swiftly finds himself involved in a diplomatic situation - and much worse off. S6 E5: From Fulham With Love
John Sullivan's bittersweet sitcom. Vince traces Penny's address and decides to pay a visit, but her mother has not forgiven him. S1 E2: Hello Again
George has been claiming for a non-existent son for the past 19 years in order to fiddle his tax returns, but he is in danger of being caught when a tax inspector visits. S4 E6: My Son, My Son
The first-ever episode of the classic sitcom. One-man disaster area Frank Spencer becomes a wholesale ironmongery salesman. S1 E1: Getting a Job
Sharon and Tracey work out that Dorien has an important birthday coming up - and decide that the event calls for a celebration whatever she thinks. S12 E8: Forever Young
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
When the Steptoes find out that the lease is running out on their house and need a bank loan to buy it, the bank manager ends up making them an offer they can't refuse. S5 E7: Men of Property

Miss Jones is collecting for a famine charity when Philip challenges Rigsby to go 48 hours without eating - Rigsby gladly accepts the offer but soon finds his will weakening. S2 E2: Food Glorious Food
Bob takes time off from marriage to join Terry on a quiet weekend's fishing - Bob is racked by guilt, but he's not the guilty party. S2 E8: Affairs and Relations
Arthur's chum Sidney comes to him for protection from a murderer, but Arthur's idea of a safe house is a caravan belonging to Dave's sister. S10 E3: All Things Brighton Beautiful
When Joyce appoints Monty as the new Entertainment Manager, he plans a magical evening at Neptune's with guest stars the Chuckle Brothers - meanwhile, the Dawsons speculate further about Cyd. S10 E3
Patrick's plans to start afresh with his new love D'Reen are scuppered when Mimi forces him to do one last job to guarantee the financial security of the family. S7 E6
When the lease is up on the flat and George wants to increase the rent, the flatmates decide that the only way to raise the money is to get a fourth tenant to share. S6 E2: One More for the Pot
When Miss Jones becomes engaged to a librarian and looks set to move out, Rigsby is distraught but is determined that no other man should have her. S2 E4: Moonlight and Roses
